title: Blackjack
tagline: The strategy-dependent one — RTP is set by how you play.
mechanic: Standard blackjack: infinite deck, dealer stands on 17, blackjack pays 3:2, hit/stand only. Unlike the ladders, there's no fair-multiplier trick — the house edge emerges from rules + player decisions. The declared 97.4% holds only under basic strategy; doubles and splits (omitted here) would add ~2%.
showcases: why a generic simulator is meaningless here
the open→close money flow can't double/split (needs a 2nd wager)
mathTest: Drive it with the reference strategy: `--strategy basic` ≈ declared RTP, while `--compare` shows hit-always / stand-always / random all UNDERperform. Generic first/random simulation reads as a 'fail' — by design.
